summary: Electric Althea finish and fiery Wheel > Other One jam
blurb: The second set rolls nonstop with a scorching Wheel → Other One sequence, an electric Althea climax, and tight jams on Wharf Rat and Throwing Stones. Jerry’s guitar is on fire, Brent’s B‑3 organ shines, and Phil’s bass finally steps out of the mix. A must‑listen for anyone after the 1982 transition period.
review: September 23, 1982 at Veterans Memorial Coliseum captured a high‑energy snapshot of the Dead in their early‑‘80s stride. The first set rides a solid groove through classics like "Greatest Story Ever Told" and a bluesy "Little Red Rooster," but it’s the second set that really ignites – the band launches into a seamless, thunder‑filled "The Wheel" → "The Other One" jam that feels like a live‑wire conduit for improvisation. The performance caps off with an electric “Althea” that leaves the house buzzing, while Brent Mydland’s B‑3 organ swells behind every transition and even adds a vocal shout on "Johnny B. Goode." Drummers Mickey Hart and Bill Kreutzmann drive a post‑drum surge that lifts the whole set, and Phil Lesh finally gets his bass up front thanks to Miller’s mastering. All told, this show is a vivid example of the band locking in during a period many fans consider underrated, making it an essential listen for Deadheads seeking both tight playing and raw excitement.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: All three recordings are flagged as positive, with multiple reviewers praising the same moments – the Wheel→Other One jam, electric Althea finish, and Brent’s organ presence. Consistent high‑voltage guitar comments for Jerry and a noted boost in Phil’s bass presence add weight to the praise, justifying a strong positive sentiment adjustment.

best_recording.reason: Soundboard source with clear, well‑balanced mix; Phil’s bass is unusually present and Brent’s organ shines, delivering the most faithful representation of the show’s energy.

key_highlights: Fiery "Wheel → Other One" jam that propels the second set
Electric climax on "Althea" with standout Jerry solo
Brent Mydland's B‑3 organ prominence and vocal shout on "Johnny B. Goode"
Post‑drum segment that lifts the band into high‑octane improvisation

band_performance.Jerry: Guitar is on fire – high‑voltage solos, especially the electric finish on "Althea," and solid vocal delivery throughout.
band_performance.Phil: Bass unusually forward in the mix for this era, giving a fuller low end that locks into the groove.
band_performance.Bob: Slide work on "Althea" praised as an improvement; contributes strong rhythmic drive during jams.
band_performance.Brent: B‑3 organ dominates with rich texture; adds vocal punch on "Johnny B. Goode," keeping keyboards front and center.
band_performance.Mickey: Drums power the post‑drum surge that fuels the second‑set momentum, delivering crisp accents and dynamic lifts.
band_performance.Billy: Pairs with Mickey to create a tight, propulsive rhythm section; his fills help drive the intense "Wheel → Other One" transition.
